rxn_class_summary: Photoelectrochemical radical selenocyclization of N-alkenylacrylamides with diselenides (PhSeSePh) catalyzed by 4CzIPN to form selenylated pyrrolinones (e.g., 3a).
electrochemical_role: Anodic oxidation regenerates/oxidizes the photocatalyst (4CzIPN) or directly oxidizes selenide to a selenyl radical; cathode reduces protons to H2 (hydrogen evolution detected), enabling net oxidative selenocyclization under combined photo/electrochemical conditions.

key_intermediates.name: carbon-centered radical from PhSe• addition to acrylamide alkene of 1a
cyclized alkyl radical after intramolecular addition onto pendant styrenyl alkene
carbocation after oxidation of cyclized radical (radical-polar crossover)

electron_flow: Visible light excites 4CzIPN; the excited photocatalyst oxidizes PhSeSePh to generate a PhSe• radical that adds to the acrylamide alkene of 1a, initiating 5-exo-trig radical cyclization onto the pendant alkene. The resulting alkyl radical is oxidized (radical-polar crossover) to a cation that undergoes deprotonation/aromatization to give 3a; the anode regenerates the photocatalyst while the cathode reduces protons to H2.

probe_reagents.name: TEMPO
probe_reagents.observation: Yield partially suppressed (60% A / 40% B / 37% C); radical pathway implicated but not fully shut down.

probe_reagents.name: BHT
probe_reagents.observation: Reaction completely suppressed (0% under all conditions), strongly supporting radical intermediates.

probe_reagents.name: 1,1-diphenylethylene (DPE)
probe_reagents.observation: Partial suppression (41/32/46%), consistent with radical intermediates.

probe_reagents.name: azulene
probe_reagents.observation: Reaction shut down (trace), suggesting triplet-state involvement.

probe_reagents.name: ferrocene (Fc)
probe_reagents.observation: Trace yield; triplet quenching suppresses reaction.

probe_reagents.name: biisobutenyl (2,5-dimethyl-2,4-hexadiene)
probe_reagents.observation: Trace under A, 31% under B; partial triplet quenching.

notes: Mechanistic SI includes quenching experiments (radical and triplet scavengers), reaction profiles, on-off light/current experiments, CV, UV-Vis, fluorescence, H2 detection, Se-scrambling crossover experiments with MeSeSeMe under 395 nm, excited-state redox potential determinations for 4CzIPN, 1a, and 3a in DCE/HFIP (3:1), and DFT calculations. Control experiments confirm 1a/3a do not act as 395-nm photooxidants or energy-transfer photocatalysts, supporting 4CzIPN as the active photocatalyst. Triplet-state involvement supported by quenching with azulene, Fc, benzil, etc.
